  "findings": [
    {
      "severity": "high",
      "category": "nil-safety/type-assertion",
      "file": "pkg/plugins/log/logger.go",
      "line": 47,
      "title": "Unsafe type assertion without nil check on log.FromContext result",
      "description": "In the grafanaInfraLogWrapper.FromContext method, the result of d.l.FromContext(ctx) is type-asserted to *log.ConcreteLogger without first checking if the underlying logger implements the necessary interface. If FromContext returns nil or a type that doesn't satisfy *log.ConcreteLogger, the ok flag will be false and the fallback d.New() is used. However, if the underlying log.FromContext itself panics or returns an unexpected type, this will fail. More critically, there is no guarantee that d.l.FromContext(ctx) won't return nil, which would cause a panic on the type assertion.",
      "suggestion": "Add a nil check before the type assertion: `if ctx == nil { return d.New() }` or ensure d.l is never nil, and verify that log.FromContext(ctx) is guaranteed to return a non-nil value."
    },
    {
      "severity": "medium",

The diff is truncated, so it's unclear if all call sites were properly updated. If any call site still passes these removed parameters, the code will fail to compile.",
